text: {{c1::Schema validation}} checks that incoming data conforms to expected columns, types, and constraints.
explanation: It prevents silent data issues from destabilizing training and inference.
usage: Essential in production ML pipelines and data quality frameworks.
spanish: La validación de esquema comprueba que los datos entrantes cumplan columnas, tipos y restricciones esperadas.

text: Data {{c1::lineage}} tracks where data comes from, how it is transformed, and where it is consumed.
explanation: It is crucial for reproducibility, auditing, and debugging model issues.
usage: Important in regulated and enterprise AI systems.
spanish: La lineage de datos rastrea de dónde provienen los datos, cómo se transforman y dónde se consumen.

scenario: Feature Stores: Online and Offline Features ⚡
text: {{c1::Feature stores}} provide both offline training features and online serving features from the same source of truth.
explanation: This prevents train/serve skew and improves consistency across environments.
usage: Common in recommendation, fraud, and personalization systems.
spanish: Los feature stores proporcionan features offline para entrenamiento y features online para serving desde la misma fuente de verdad.

text: A/{{c1::B testing}} compares two versions of a system by randomizing users and measuring business or model outcomes.
explanation: It is the gold standard for validating product changes and algorithmic interventions.
usage: Widely used in ranking, search, and product experimentation.
spanish: El A/B testing compara dos versiones de un sistema randomizando usuarios y midiendo resultados de negocio o del modelo.

text: Statistical power measures how likely an experiment is to detect a {{c1::real}} effect when one exists.
explanation: Low-powered tests waste time and can produce false negatives.
usage: Important for experiment design, budget planning, and launch decisions.
spanish: La potencia estadística mide qué tan probable es que un experimento detecte un efecto real cuando existe.

text: Train-{{c1::serve skew}} occurs when the features used during training differ from those used at inference time.
explanation: It is a frequent cause of unexpected performance drops in production ML.
usage: Critical for real-time scoring, search, and personalization systems.
spanish: El train-serve skew ocurre cuando las features usadas durante entrenamiento difieren de las usadas en inferencia.

text:
Pregunta: Which storage engine does Vertex AI Feature Store use to achieve sub-millisecond p99 latencies for online feature serving?
Opciones:
  • A) Bigtable (for low latency online feature serving)
  • B) BigQuery (for historical analytical lookups)
  • C) Cloud Storage (for batch pipeline reads)
  • D) Cloud SQL (for relational feature constraints)

Respuesta correcta: {{c1::A) Bigtable (for low latency online feature serving)}}
explanation: Justificación: Vertex AI Feature Store leverages Cloud Bigtable as the backend engine for serving online features in real-time, caching feature views to support massive request-per-second (RPS) throughput with extremely low latency.
usage: Quiz assertion: A) Bigtable (for low latency online feature serving)
spanish: Quiz: Which storage engine does Vertex AI Feature Store use to achieve sub-millisecond p99 latencies for online feature serving?

text: To deduplicate raw text at web scale for LLM pretraining, pipelines use MinHash to estimate Jaccard similarity and {{c1::Locality Sensitive Hashing (LSH)}} to group candidate pairs into buckets.
explanation: MinHash estimates Jaccard similarity of n-grams between documents. Locality Sensitive Hashing (LSH) hashes these signatures into buckets, ensuring highly similar documents hash to the same bucket with high probability, reducing candidate comparison complexity.
usage: Used in web-scale pre-processing pipelines (e.g., LLaMA, Pile) to filter billions of documents.
spanish: Para eliminar duplicados de texto a escala web para preentrenamiento de LLM, los pipelines usan MinHash para estimar similitud de Jaccard y Locality Sensitive Hashing (LSH) para agrupar pares candidatos en buckets.
